title: Project Management Office - PMO (all gender)

company_info.size_raw: not found in JD text; orchestrator context: large engineering/IT consulting company

required_skills: Programme/project management of complex technical programmes
Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) planning, milestones, dependencies
Risk and opportunity management, escalation templates
Steering Committee management and status reporting
bonus_skills: Cost and time structure planning in bid/proposal context
matched_skills: Risk escalation and prioritisation management (HSBC PMO)
Steering Committee preparation and management (HSBC PMO)
C-level / C-suite reporting and data storytelling (HSBC PMO)
Programme planning and multi-workstream coordination (HSBC USD 8M programme)
KPI framework design and performance reporting (Hapag-Lloyd)
missing_skills: Formal PM certification (PMP, PRINCE2, or German equivalent — not listed in CV)
key_requirements: Build and maintain programme planning including WBS, milestones, dependencies and resource planning
Own risk and opportunity management including escalation templates and mitigation actions
Manage Steering Committee meetings, status reporting, decision-making and escalation logic
Collaborate closely with Systems Architect, Commercial & Subcontract Manager, and Requirements & Interface Manager
Support the proposal/bid phase with time/cost structure, assumptions, process management and deliverable planning
cv_highlights: Led PMO operations for USD 8M digital transformation programme at HSBC — directly matches programme governance scale
Prepared Steering Committee materials and managed C-suite reporting cadence at HSBC
Risk escalation and prioritisation management cited as core skill
Cross-functional coordination across multiple workstreams at HSBC and Hapag-Lloyd
Fluent German — essential for this German-language role in Bremen
Power BI certified — strong for data-driven status reporting
gaps: No engineering/technical programme context (ALTEN serves aerospace, automotive, defense — Boru's background is banking/logistics digital transformation)
No PM certification listed (JD explicitly requires 'Zusatzqualifikationen im Projektmanagement')
No proposal or bid management experience (a distinct listed responsibility)
Bremen is ~130km from Lüneburg — relocation or long commute required; JD mentions 'mobiles Arbeiten nach Absprache' (hybrid by agreement only)
No mention of WBS tooling or scheduling software (MS Project, Primavera etc.)
recommended_emphasis: Reframe HSBC PMO as complex multi-workstream programme governance with budget accountability (USD 8M)
Highlight Steering Committee ownership and C-suite escalation management as direct experience match
Emphasise risk escalation framework design and prioritisation methodology
Stress German language proficiency prominently — this is a German-language role
If any proposal/RFP involvement exists at HSBC or Hapag-Lloyd, surface it explicitly
Consider adding PMP/PRINCE2 as in-progress or planned to address certification gap
recommendation: Weak match (45/100). Core PMO competencies align well — Steering Committee, risk escalation, C-suite reporting, multi-workstream coordination — but two structural gaps significantly reduce fit: (1) ALTEN's clients are in engineering/aerospace/automotive/defense and the JD requires 'fundierte Erfahrung im Programm-/Projektmanagement komplexer technischer Vorhaben', which Boru's banking and logistics background does not clearly demonstrate; (2) the JD explicitly requires PM additional qualifications (PMP/PRINCE2 or equivalent) which Boru does not list. The location gap (130km) adds further friction. Apply only if Boru can credibly address the technical programme context gap in the cover letter and has any PM certification to cite.
